title: ecb / climate risk / sovereign bonds | Blog Post | Climate-related disasters can push up the cost of debt
summary: ECB staff research finds that climate risk is increasingly priced into sovereign bond markets, with transition risk (measured by carbon emissions intensity) showing a significant positive relationship with ten-year sovereign bond yields. The effect is more pronounced for developing economies, and highly indebted countries face more pronounced yield increases following climate disaster shocks. Effective climate policies can help mitigate increases in debt servicing costs.
